user_query:
I'm a privacy‑minded writer and I want to sanity‑check that my note app isn't phoning home before I start drafting. Using the mock snapshot under input/, please do an offline privacy audit and harden copies of the configs without touching the originals.

What I need:
1) Inspect all files under input/scribo and input/system to identify privacy‑relevant settings, background services, and processes. Treat the following as privacy concerns to flag:
   - In input/scribo/config.ini: [telemetry].enabled, [sync].cloud_enabled, [privacy].redact_drafts, [logging].level, [crash].sentry.
   - In input/system/services.yaml: services.crash_reporter.enabled and services.metrics_collector.enabled.
   - In input/system/processes.txt: any process line containing "metrics", "sync", "telemetry", or "crash" (case‑insensitive).
   - In input/scribo/logs/app.log: log lines indicating outbound activity such as "Uploading", "Syncing", or "Sentry" (case‑insensitive) to use as evidence.

2) Write a structured audit to output/audit/privacy_findings.json. For each issue you find, include an object with fields: issue_type (one of telemetry|cloud_sync|crash_reporting|verbose_logging|redaction|network_process|service), source_file (relative path), key_or_item (setting or process/service name), current_value (string), recommended_value (string), evidence (a representative matched key or line), and action ("modified" if your hardened copy changes it, otherwise "flagged_only"). Also include a top‑level array scanned_files listing every file you inspected.

3) Create hardened copies of the configs under output/hardened/ with these changes applied, preserving directory structure and leaving input/ untouched:
   - output/hardened/scribo/config.ini: set [telemetry].enabled=false, [sync].cloud_enabled=false, [privacy].redact_drafts=true, [logging].level="warn", and [crash].sentry="off" if those keys exist. Leave other keys as is.
   - output/hardened/system/services.yaml: set services.crash_reporter.enabled=false and services.metrics_collector.enabled=false. Leave services.auto_update unchanged.

4) Write output/audit/changes.json capturing only the changes you made. Structure it as a mapping from each modified file path to a list of change objects with fields: key (e.g., "telemetry.enabled"), old_value, new_value, and rationale (one‑line reason tied to privacy).

Please rely only on the provided files, and keep the original input files unmodified. The deliverables I will check are: output/audit/privacy_findings.json, output/audit/changes.json, output/hardened/scribo/config.ini, and output/hardened/system/services.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
